pages.title: Knowledge base — index
pages.excerpt: Knowledge base A survey of the context-management and context-repo landscape (mid-2026). What this KB is and who owns it: kb-card.md. How to work here: AGENTS.md. Start here The context repo — the idea this whole KB (and kit) is about.…

pages.title: Knowledge base — change log
pages.excerpt: Change log Newest first. Entry format: ## YYYY-MM-DD heading, bulleted changes, each naming the operation (ingest lint setup edit) and the pages touched. 2026-07-18 setup Bundle scaffolded: index.md, log.md, type templates (note, source, decision, topic, standard, tool).

pages.title: Open Knowledge Format (OKF)
pages.summary: Google's v0.1 draft spec for knowledge bundles — markdown + YAML frontmatter, type as the only required key.

frontmatter.title: Open Knowledge Format (OKF)
frontmatter.summary: Google's v0.1 draft spec for knowledge bundles — markdown + YAML frontmatter, type as the only required key.

pages.excerpt: Open Knowledge Format (OKF) OKF is the format anchor of this kit. It specifies a knowledge bundle: a directory of markdown files with YAML frontmatter, where type is the only required key. "Format, not platform" — no schema registry, no…

pages.title: Templates — index
pages.excerpt: Entity types One file per type; the file both defines the type and serves as its template. Types are grown, not designed: add one only when ingestion keeps producing pages that don't fit. Adding a type = add a file…

pages.title: decision
pages.summary: A choice that was made — what, why, what was rejected.

frontmatter.summary: A choice that was made — what, why, what was rejected.

pages.excerpt: decision Capture decisions where they happened, not in slide decks. Frontmatter should carry date and, when known, owner. Body: context, the decision, options rejected and why, and links to affected pages. Decisions are immutable once made — a reversal is…

pages.title: note
pages.summary: Default type — one distilled piece of knowledge, one file.

frontmatter.summary: Default type — one distilled piece of knowledge, one file.

pages.excerpt: note The default. Use when nothing more specific fits. One topic per file; if a note grows two topics, split it and keep the links. A draft note is a placeholder someone should improve; solid means a human stands behind…

pages.title: source
pages.summary: An external artifact (article, thread, spec, paper) ingested into this KB.

frontmatter.summary: An external artifact (article, thread, spec, paper) ingested into this KB.

pages.excerpt: source One page per ingested artifact. Frontmatter should carry url and date (of the artifact, not the ingest). Body: a distilled summary first, then a ## Raw or ## Key excerpts section with verbatim material — never edit inside that…

pages.title: standard
pages.summary: A published spec or convention (demo type — remove via setup if unneeded).

frontmatter.summary: A published spec or convention (demo type — remove via setup if unneeded).

pages.excerpt: standard For specs, formats, and conventions. Frontmatter should carry steward (who governs it), statusnote (draft/stable/adopted), and url. Body: what it specifies, adoption reality vs claims, and how it relates to neighboring standards (links).

pages.title: tool
pages.summary: A product or system in the landscape (demo type — remove via setup if unneeded).

frontmatter.summary: A product or system in the landscape (demo type — remove via setup if unneeded).

pages.excerpt: tool For products, platforms, and systems. Frontmatter should carry maker and url. Body: what it does, the idea it embodies (link the topic page), and its relationship to the standards it implements or ignores.

pages.title: topic
pages.summary: A concept page synthesizing what this KB knows about one idea.

frontmatter.summary: A concept page synthesizing what this KB knows about one idea.

pages.excerpt: topic The synthesis layer: written from multiple sources, linking to each. A topic page answers "what do we know about X and how sure are we?" — cite sources inline, surface disagreements between them as content. Update topic pages on…

pages.title: Agent memory vs. knowledge bases
pages.summary: Memory systems and KBs are merging into context repos; ChatGPT's opaque memory is the philosophical outlier.

frontmatter.title: Agent memory vs. knowledge bases
frontmatter.summary: Memory systems and KBs are merging into context repos; ChatGPT's opaque memory is the philosophical outlier.

pages.excerpt: Agent memory vs. knowledge bases Agent memory systems (what an agent remembers across sessions) and knowledge bases (curated, shared knowledge) started as separate product categories. The mid-2026 signal is that they are merging — into context repos. The clearest evidence…

pages.title: Context engineering
pages.summary: The discipline of filling the context window with the right information — the frame that displaced prompt engineering.

frontmatter.title: Context engineering
frontmatter.summary: The discipline of filling the context window with the right information — the frame that displaced prompt engineering.

pages.excerpt: Context engineering Context engineering is the umbrella discipline this KB's whole subject sits under. Karpathy's June 2025 framing named it: "the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information" — and the term displaced…

pages.title: The context repo
pages.summary: A durable, versioned knowledge substrate shared by humans and agents — the idea this KB surveys.

frontmatter.title: The context repo
frontmatter.summary: A durable, versioned knowledge substrate shared by humans and agents — the idea this KB surveys.

pages.excerpt: The context repo A context repo is a version-controlled collection of markdown files that serves as the durable knowledge substrate for both humans and agents: readable without tooling, parseable without SDKs, diffable in git, portable across agent stacks. This repo's…

pages.title: KB discovery and the kb-card gap
pages.summary: No adopted standard describes a knowledge base the way a model card describes a model — the gap kb-kit's kb-card targets.

frontmatter.title: KB discovery and the kb-card gap
frontmatter.summary: No adopted standard describes a knowledge base the way a model card describes a model — the gap kb-kit's kb-card targets.

pages.excerpt: KB discovery and the kb-card gap How does an agent (or a team) find a knowledge base, and decide whether to trust it? As of mid-2026, no dedicated, widely adopted "KB card" standard exists. That is the open gap in…

pages.title: The LLM-wiki pattern
pages.summary: Karpathy's three-layer, agent-maintained markdown wiki — ingest/query/lint as the loop, drift as the failure mode.

frontmatter.title: The LLM-wiki pattern
frontmatter.summary: Karpathy's three-layer, agent-maintained markdown wiki — ingest/query/lint as the loop, drift as the failure mode.

pages.excerpt: The LLM-wiki pattern Karpathy's April 2026 gist described the pattern this kit implements: instead of RAG retrieval at query time, "the LLM incrementally builds and maintains a persistent wiki" — a "persistent, compounding artifact" sitting between you and the raw…

pages.title: Progressive disclosure
pages.summary: The shared mechanism across skills, OKF, CLAUDE.md, and MCP — load near-zero tokens until knowledge is actually needed.

frontmatter.title: Progressive disclosure
frontmatter.summary: The shared mechanism across skills, OKF, CLAUDE.md, and MCP — load near-zero tokens until knowledge is actually needed.

pages.excerpt: Progressive disclosure Progressive disclosure is the one mechanism every surviving context standard shares: expose a cheap summary layer first, and load the expensive full content only when it is actually needed. It is the survey's second cross-cutting takeaway (after "markdown-in-git…

pages.title: kb-card: a model card for knowledge bases
pages.excerpt: kb-card: a model card for knowledge bases A kb-card is a single file, kb-card.md, at the root of a repository that contains a knowledge base. It does for KBs what a model card does for models: lets a human or…

pages.title: kb-card — kb-kit demo knowledge base
pages.summary: A survey of the context-management and context-repo landscape (mid-2026), shipped as the working demo content of the kb-kit starter kit.

frontmatter.name: kb-kit demo knowledge base
frontmatter.description: A survey of the context-management and context-repo landscape (mid-2026), shipped as the working demo content of the kb-kit starter kit.
frontmatter.scope: Standards, tools, patterns, and ideas for giving AI agents and humans a shared, versioned knowledge substrate. Out of scope — general AI news, model benchmarks, anything not about context/knowledge management.

pages.excerpt: kb-card — kb-kit demo knowledge base This card describes the demo KB that ships with kb-kit so the site, search, and skills have something real to work on. When you fork the kit, the setup skill replaces this card with…
